title: Skill Garden
summary: A visually distinctive portfolio concept where projects are generated into an interactive 3D garden from structured local data. Built to combine recruiter-friendly presentation with WebGPU-first rendering, procedural generation, instancing, and interactive exploration.
contributions: Designed and implemented the entire concept end-to-end
Built the rendering setup with WebGPU as primary renderer and WebGL fallback
Created data-driven scene generation, interaction logic, and portfolio presentation flow
challenge: Combining visual presentation, data-driven generation, rendering fallback, and performance-conscious structure in one coherent portfolio experience.

title: VA Chat Service
summary: A TypeScript-based AI chat service for exchange-information workflows, designed around streaming responses, service-to-service security, and a RAG-ready architecture with future retrieval and ingestion support.
role: Project team member, primary builder of the service
contributions: Implemented the service architecture and core chat endpoint
Built SSE token streaming and service-level HMAC protection
Worked on the RAG-oriented foundation including embeddings and vector database direction
challenge: Designing a clean AI service foundation that supports secure streaming and future retrieval features without overcomplicating the first implementation.

title: Go Exchange Client
summary: A student exchange information platform designed to make destination research easier and support discovery through an AI assistant. The client combines product UI work with AI-oriented information access.
contributions: Worked on AI-facing product functionality and selected frontend areas
Built backend-facing routes and integration paths for AI features
Contributed to vector database development for retrieval-augmented workflows
challenge: Connecting a practical user-facing product with AI-assisted search and retrieval in a way that stays genuinely useful.

title: Go Exchange Server
summary: Backend services for the Go Exchange platform, built with a microservices-inspired structure. The project combines authentication, content management, upload handling, and AI-related routing in a service-oriented architecture.
contributions: Worked on AI-related routing and backend integration
Contributed to authentication-related functionality
Handled security-sensitive request flow concerns around HMAC and streamed responses
challenge: Implementing secure request handling and safe SSE parsing while keeping the service architecture practical and maintainable.

title: PawPics
summary: A social media style school project built with Next.js, focused on modern UI, content interaction, and user-facing product features. The project includes uploads, responsive layouts, and a component-driven frontend structure.
contributions: Built the project independently from UI structure to feature implementation
Worked with routing, reusable UI patterns, uploads, and responsive layouts
Focused on making the app feel like a real product instead of a minimal exercise
challenge: Handling Next.js routing and tying together multiple UI and product-level features in a coherent way.

title: Weedify Frontend
summary: A mobile recipe-sharing application where users can discover, save, rate, comment on, and share recipes. Built as a team project with React Native and focused on mobile UX, content interaction, forms, and API-connected features.
contributions: Contributed across the frontend as part of a team project
Worked with API-connected mobile features, forms, and state handling
Learned and applied React Native in a more product-like mobile context
challenge: Working effectively in a team while learning a newer mobile stack and keeping the user experience consistent across features.

title: Weedify Backend
summary: Backend services for a recipe-sharing application, covering authentication, user profiles, recipe content, comments, ratings, favorites, follows, notifications, and media upload workflows.
contributions: Contributed to backend development as part of the project team
Worked in an environment involving auth flows, content features, and media-related logic
Gained experience from building a broader feature set across multiple connected backend concerns
challenge: Managing a larger backend feature surface that includes authentication, content actions, and user-driven interactions in one application.
